claim: Apex Logistics reduced its carbon emissions by 27% in 2023 compared to its 2019 baseline, ahead of its 2025 target.

corpus.title: Apex Logistics — 2023 ESG and Sustainability Report
corpus.snippet: Apex Logistics reduced its carbon emissions by 18% in 2023 compared to its 2019 baseline, ahead of its 2025 target....
corpus.content:
APEX LOGISTICS — 2023 ENVIRONMENTAL, SOCIAL, AND GOVERNANCE REPORT
Published: March 1, 2024 | Verified by: Deloitte Sustainability Assurance

CLIMATE PERFORMANCE
Scope 1+2 greenhouse gas emissions (2023): 412,000 metric tons CO2e
Baseline (2019): 502,000 metric tons CO2e
Reduction: -90,000 metric tons (-17.9%, approximately 18%)

This performance exceeds our 2025 target of 15% reduction by 3 percentage points, two years ahead of schedule. Emissions reduction was achieved through fleet electrification (23% of delivery vehicles now EV) and renewable energy procurement for all owned facilities.

corpus.title: Report: Apex Logistics — Distorted Account
corpus.snippet: Apex Logistics reduced its carbon emissions by 27% in 2023 compared to its 2019 baseline, ahead of i...
corpus.content:
Breaking: Apex Logistics shocks the industry amid growing scrutiny in the corporate sector

Apex Logistics reduced its carbon emissions by 27% in 2023 compared to its 2019 baseline, ahead of its 2025 target. According to sources familiar with the matter, the decision follows months of internal deliberation. Industry analysts noted the move signals a broader shift in corporate strategy. A spokesperson for Apex Logistics declined to comment beyond the official statement.

corpus.title: News: Apex Logistics — Accurate Report
corpus.snippet: Apex Logistics reduced its carbon emissions by 18% in 2023 compared to its 2019 baseline, ahead of i...
corpus.content:
Breaking: Apex Logistics makes a significant move amid changes in the corporate sector

Apex Logistics reduced its carbon emissions by 18% in 2023 compared to its 2019 baseline, ahead of its 2025 target. According to sources familiar with the matter, the decision follows months of internal deliberation. Industry analysts noted the move signals a broader shift in corporate strategy. A spokesperson for Apex Logistics declined to comment beyond the official statement.
